content:
As you revisit your past writings, you can observe the evolution of your ideas over time, track your progress, and identify areas where your thinking has matured or changed. This self-reflection promotes metacognition, making you more aware of your thought processes and biases. Moreover, writing often requires research and exploration, leading you to discover new information and perspectives. This continuous learning process enriches your thinking, broadens your knowledge base, and helps you become more open-minded. In summary, honing your writing skills is not just about becoming a better communicator; it's a pathway to becoming a more adept and insightful thinker.

Watch the following video to explore how writing can sharpen your thinking!



After you finish this page, click Next to complete the Week 6 Weekly Writing Warm-Up.


Week 6 | Weekly Writing Warm-Up

Weekly Writing Warm-Up
Introduction
Due by the end of the week.

Welcome to the Weekly Writing Warm-Up, where you will practice the art of writing. For many of us, the extent of our writing happens in text over our phones, so writing papers and essays feels awkward. To this end, we will participate in Weekly Writing Warm-Ups. These warm-ups are less about what you write and more about putting your fingers on your computer keys. You will not be critiqued on your paragraph or paper structure, but the content does need to be clear. Consistent writing can be a valuable tool to enhance your communication skills, creativity, and self-expression. Each week, you will be given new instructions (see below) to reflect on and write about. This warm-up activity does not require prework, outlines, or drafts.
